text: ‘Ephraim’ is probably used here to represent the people of Israel, as often in the prophets, who contrast it with ‘Juda’ (cf. verse 67). The reference may be to the refusal to invade Palestine (Num. 14), but the chronology of this psalm is difficult to disentangle.
Literally ‘of the strong’; but the Angels are probably meant; cf.Wisd.16.10.
